event.name: 3rd International Workshop on Plastic Pollution in the Oceans
event.fullName: 3rd International Workshop on Plastic Pollution in the Oceans (IWPPO) - Environmental Policies and Technological Innovations: Strategies for Reducing Plastic Pollution in the Pan-Amazon Region
event.theme: Environmental Policies and Technological Innovations: Strategies for Reducing Plastic Pollution in the Pan-Amazon Region
event.shortTheme: Plastic pollution, Pan-Amazon Region, environmental policies and technological innovation
event.description: The 3rd International Workshop on Plastic Pollution in the Oceans consolidates an international space for coordination between science, public policy, and innovation. The 2026 edition will be held in Manaus, focusing on the Pan-Amazon Region and bringing together researchers, government representatives, civil society organizations, the productive sector, young researchers, and communities impacted by plastic pollution.

hero.title: International Workshop on Plastic Pollution in the Oceans
hero.highlight: Pan-Amazon Region, science, and innovation against plastic pollution
hero.description: The 2026 edition connects rivers, forests, oceans, biodiversity, environmental policies, and emerging technologies to discuss strategies for reducing plastic pollution in the Pan-Amazon Region.

about.title: The Pan-Amazon Region at the center of the debate on plastic pollution
paragraphs: The 3rd IWPPO proposes an international dialogue space focused on building solutions for plastic pollution in Amazonian aquatic and marine-coastal environments.

The event seeks to strengthen cooperation between science, government, organizations, the productive sector, and society by promoting debates on monitoring, tracking, recycling, circular economy, and environmental policies.

The 2026 edition will be held in Manaus, placing the environmental challenges of the Pan-Amazon Region at the center of the discussion. This region is strategic for the conservation of biodiversity, rivers, forests, and ecosystems connected to the oceans.

history.title: History of IWPPO
paragraphs: IWPPO emerged from the need to find solutions to plastic pollution in water bodies, especially seas and oceans.
The 1st IWPPO took place in November 2024 in Curitiba, Paraná, at PUC-Curitiba, under the theme ‘Challenges and Solutions for Monitoring and Recycling Plastic Production to Protect the Oceans.’
The 2nd IWPPO took place in September 2025 in Maceió, Alagoas, under the theme ‘Oceans in crisis, science with purpose: transforming knowledge into hope.’
The IWPPO proposal was born from discussions held at the 6th Congress of Environmental Studies, promoted in 2023 by the Graduate Program in Environmental Systems Analysis at Cesmac University Center.
Since its first editions, the event has sought to integrate teaching, research, outreach, innovation, and dialogue between academia, society, the productive sector, and public authorities.

justification.title: Justification
paragraphs: Holding the 3rd International Workshop on Plastic Pollution in the Oceans in Manaus is a strategic initiative in light of the environmental and socioeconomic challenges related to plastic pollution in the Pan-Amazon Region.

The Pan-Amazon Region spans different Latin American countries and faces increasing impacts caused by the improper disposal of plastic waste, affecting aquatic and terrestrial environments, traditional communities, and Indigenous peoples.

The event proposes integration between science, public policy, technological innovation, and local knowledge, bringing together researchers, policymakers, civil society representatives, the private sector, and local communities.
The choice of the Pan-Amazon Region is strategic because the region requires coordinated, specific actions that are sensitive to its environmental, social, cultural, and economic particularities.
The 3rd IWPPO seeks to foster international and regional cooperation, guide public policies, stimulate the circular economy, and support the use of technologies aimed at reducing plastic pollution.

professionalContribution.title: Contribution to professionals involved in the event
paragraphs: The 3rd IWPPO offers professionals involved in the event an opportunity for scientific and technological updating on the monitoring, mitigation, tracking, recycling, and management of plastic pollution.
The event promotes interdisciplinarity by bringing together researchers, public managers, representatives of the productive sector, social organizations, young researchers, and local communities.

The program encourages the creation and strengthening of international and regional cooperation networks, expanding scientific partnerships, public policies, and private initiatives aimed at reducing plastic pollution.

The workshop also contributes to the training of professionals prepared to work in environmental management, circular economy, technological innovation, governance, and environmental policies applied to the Amazonian reality.

Valuing the knowledge of traditional communities and Indigenous peoples broadens the reach of environmental solutions, ensuring that the fight against plastic pollution considers the social, cultural, and territorial contexts of the Pan-Amazon Region.

thematicAxes.title: Environmental Policies
thematicAxes.description: Discussion on regulatory frameworks, environmental governance, economic instruments, and public strategies for reducing plastic pollution.

thematicAxes.title: Technological Innovation
thematicAxes.description: Application of emerging technologies, traceability, remote monitoring, artificial intelligence, sensors, drones, and Industry 4.0 solutions.

thematicAxes.title: Circular Economy
thematicAxes.description: Debates on reuse, recycling, reverse logistics, bioplastics, bioremediation, and new sustainable production models.

thematicAxes.title: Knowledge and Environmental Justice
thematicAxes.description: Valuing traditional communities, Indigenous peoples, environmental education, and social participation in the protection of aquatic ecosystems.

participatingPrograms.description: The participation of the Graduate Program in Production Engineering at Paulista University, through the Production and Environment Laboratory, strengthens the discussion on sustainable production, technological innovation, reuse of plastic waste, and strategies to reduce plastic pollution.

participatingPrograms.program: Environmental Sciences and Sustainability in the Amazon
participatingPrograms.description: The program contributes expertise on Amazonian environmental challenges, sustainability, conservation, public policies, waste management, and solutions specific to the reality of the region.

participatingPrograms.program: Biological Diversity and Conservation in the Tropics
participatingPrograms.description: The program contributes research on biodiversity, conservation, human impacts on tropical ecosystems, and the effects of plastic pollution on aquatic and terrestrial environments.

participatingPrograms.institution: Federal University of Alagoas - Arapiraca Campus
participatingPrograms.description: The program contributes environmental studies focused on sustainable development, waste management, innovation, and the formation of academic and social collaboration networks.
The program contributes technical and scientific knowledge on pollutants in aquatic environments, monitoring of coastal and marine systems, and studies on microplastics.

The program contributes an interdisciplinary approach to policy, environmental management, sustainability, territory, society, consumption, and the formulation of strategies to reduce plastic pollution.

activities.title: Environmental Cinema
activities.description: Educational initiative aimed at raising awareness among basic education students in Manaus about environmental preservation and the impacts of plastic pollution on rivers, lagoons, and oceans.

activities.title: Registration
activities.description: Distribution of participant kits to attendees.

activities.title: Official Opening of the 3rd IWPPO
activities.description: Official opening ceremony of the event.

activities.title: Opening Keynote Lecture: The Global Challenge of Plastic Pollution and Commitments in the Pan-Amazon Region
activities.description: The lecture will address the serious problem of plastic pollution, highlighting its global and regional impacts, especially in the Pan-Amazon Region.

activities.title: Opening Coffee Break
activities.description: A moment for interaction and networking among participants.

activities.title: Cultural Presentation
activities.description: Cultural presentation connecting Amazonian culture and environmental preservation.

activities.title: Room 1 Panel: Scientific evidence on the impacts of plastic pollution in the Pan-Amazon Region
activities.description: Presentation of recent research on the impacts of plastic pollution in the Pan-Amazon Region, including contamination of rivers, soils, local ecosystems, and effects on biodiversity and riverside communities.

activities.title: Room 2 Panel: Remote monitoring, bioindicators, and waste traceability
activities.description: Discussion on technologies and methodologies for monitoring plastic pollution, including satellites and drones.

activities.title: Coffee Break
activities.description: A moment for interaction and networking among participants.

activities.title: Technical session for young researchers: poster presentations
activities.description: Session dedicated to the presentation of innovative research related to plastic pollution, its impacts, and possible solutions.

activities.title: Lunch break
activities.description: Lunch break.

activities.title: International Keynote: Emerging capture and recycling technologies
activities.description: Presentation of innovations for collecting plastics from the oceans and transforming these materials into new raw materials.

activities.title: Panel 1: Bioremediation, enzymes, and bioplastics startups
activities.description: Discussion on bioremediation, the use of enzymes in plastic degradation, and innovations from startups focused on bioplastics.

activities.title: Coffee Break
activities.description: A moment for interaction and networking among participants.

activities.title: Panel 2: Circular economy applied to river basins and ocean basins
activities.description: Debate on the circular economy as a strategy for the sustainable management of river basins and ocean basins.

activities.title: Cultural Presentation
activities.description: Theatrical presentation on plastic pollution, local culture, and environmental preservation in the Pan-Amazon Region.

activities.title: Round Table 2: Economic instruments: reverse logistics, taxation, and environmental credits
activities.description: Discussion on economic instruments to combat plastic pollution, including reverse logistics, taxation, and environmental credits.

activities.title: Round Table 1: The UN Global Plastic Agreement and developments in the Pan-Amazon Region
activities.description: Discussion on the UN Global Plastic Agreement and its implications for the Pan-Amazon Region.

activities.title: Coffee Break
activities.description: A moment for interaction and networking among participants.

activities.title: Room 2 Community Panel: Amazonian Toolkit for Action Against Plastic
activities.description: Discussion on awareness strategies, waste reduction practices, and environmental education initiatives.

activities.title: Room 1 Community Panel: Indigenous knowledge and environmental justice
activities.description: Debate on Indigenous knowledge, environmental justice, and sustainable practices for protecting aquatic ecosystems.

activities.title: Lunch break
activities.description: Lunch break.

activities.title: Panel 2: Industry 4.0, traceability, and reverse logistics
activities.description: Discussion on IoT, big data, and artificial intelligence applied to waste management, traceability, and reverse logistics.

activities.title: Coffee Break
activities.description: A moment for interaction and networking among participants.

activities.title: Working group: drafting a policy brief
activities.description: Activity focused on preparing a policy brief with practical recommendations for governments, organizations, and communities.

activities.title: Awards session
activities.description: Recognition of the best contributions in papers, posters, and innovative prototypes.

activities.title: Closing: Scientific Letter of the 3rd IWPPO
activities.description: Presentation of the Scientific Letter of the 3rd IWPPO, bringing together the event’s conclusions and recommendations.
